software_item.name: rule-engine-module
software_item.description: Generic rule engine that evaluates clinical triage rules to determine patient urgency, care recommendations, and appointment prioritization. Executes declarative YAML-based rules against patient attributes to produce clinical outcomes.
software_item.safety_class_rationale: Software that can contribute to hazardous situations (incorrect triage recommendations, wrong urgency levels) but is not the sole cause of harm. Clinical decisions depend on multiple factors including practitioner review and patient self-reporting. However, incorrect rule evaluation could lead to delayed care or inappropriate care levels.

hazard_contributions.description: Rule condition evaluates incorrectly due to expression syntax error or type mismatch
hazard_contributions.cause: Rule expression contains invalid syntax, references non-existent attributes, or performs invalid type operations (e.g., mathematical operations on strings)
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient receives incorrect triage recommendation (too urgent or not urgent enough), leading to delayed care for serious conditions or unnecessary emergency care for minor conditions

hazard_contributions.description: Rule outcome modifies immutable clinical attribute
hazard_contributions.cause: Rule attempts to modify an attribute marked as immutable (e.g., patient age, urgency level already set) after it has been initialized
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Clinical decision inconsistency where critical patient attributes are unexpectedly changed, leading to incorrect care recommendations

hazard_contributions.description: Mandatory patient attribute missing from input
hazard_contributions.cause: Input data does not include required clinical attributes (e.g., patient age, symptoms, urgency level) needed for safe rule evaluation
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Rule engine cannot execute safely, defaults to incorrect recommendation, or fails silently allowing patient to proceed without proper triage

hazard_contributions.description: Rule outcome uses incorrect operator for attribute type
hazard_contributions.cause: Rule definition uses invalid operator for attribute base type (e.g., APPEND on integer, COPY with type mismatch)
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Rule execution fails or produces malformed output, resulting in missing or corrupted clinical recommendations

hazard_contributions.cause: Input or rule outcome produces value outside allowed range (e.g., priority > max, disallowed string value) or violates type constraints
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Clinical data integrity compromised, downstream systems receive invalid values causing processing failures or incorrect care routing

hazard_contributions.description: Rules execute in incorrect order due to package sequencing
hazard_contributions.cause: Multiple rule packages executed in wrong sequence, causing later rules to override critical clinical decisions made by earlier rules
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient receives conflicting or incorrect care recommendation as critical triage rules are overridden by less specific rules

hazard_contributions.description: Remote rule repository fails to load or update rules
hazard_contributions.cause: Object storage unavailable, network failure, or corrupted rule YAML files prevent loading of current clinical rules
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: System operates with outdated or missing triage rules, potentially providing obsolete clinical guidance or failing to execute

hazard_contributions.description: Rule definition contains duplicate rule IDs within package
hazard_contributions.cause: Rule package YAML contains multiple rules with same ID, causing ambiguity in rule execution and testing
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Unpredictable rule execution where wrong version of rule executes, leading to incorrect clinical outcomes

hazard_contributions.description: String template expressions fail to resolve or contain invalid syntax
hazard_contributions.cause: Dynamic text templates reference non-existent attributes or contain malformed expressions, failing at runtime during text generation
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient or practitioner receives incomplete or malformed care instructions (e.g., 'Contact {provider.name:}' instead of actual provider name)

hazard_contributions.description: COPY operator copies from non-existent or null attribute
hazard_contributions.cause: Rule uses COPY operator to copy value from attribute that is not present in current context or is null
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Clinical attribute cleared when it should have been populated, resulting in missing urgency level, priority, or care instructions

hazard_contributions.description: Rule verification fails but execution proceeds with invalid rules
hazard_contributions.cause: Rule validation errors are logged but not enforced, allowing syntactically invalid rules to be deployed and executed
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Runtime failures during patient triage, causing service interruption or execution with undefined behavior

hazard_contributions.description: Type conversion fails for attribute values
hazard_contributions.cause: Attribute value in input data or rule outcome cannot be converted to expected type (e.g., string '23a' to integer)
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Rule execution fails or produces unexpected results due to type mismatch, leading to incomplete triage or incorrect recommendations

risk_controls_implemented.description: Expression syntax validation catches runtime evaluation errors
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleEngine.when() wraps expression evaluation in try-catch, converts ExecutionError to RuleEngineFaults with rule ID context

risk_controls_implemented.description: Immutable attribute protection prevents modification after initialization
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleEngine.executeOutcomes() checks AttributeRegistry.isImmutable() before allowing field modification, throws RuleEngineFaults if attempted

risk_controls_implemented.description: Mandatory attribute validation enforced at input
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleEngine.assertInput() validates all mandatory attributes are present using AttributeRegistry.getMandatoryAttributes(), collects faults and throws before execution

risk_controls_implemented.description: Operator-type compatibility validation at rule load time
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleOutcome.validateOutcome() checks operator.validate(baseType) and operator.validate(valueType) ensuring operator supports attribute type, fails fast before runtime
AttributeRegistryImpl.validate() checks all constraints (allowedValues, min/max) on both input and output values, throws RuleEngineFaults for violations

risk_controls_implemented.description: Output validation after each rule execution
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleEngine.execute() calls attributeRegistry.validate(output) after each rule execution, ensuring accumulated output remains valid throughout chain

risk_controls_implemented.description: Duplicate rule ID detection at package validation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleVerifier.validate() maintains Set of rule IDs, adds Fault for duplicates, prevents ambiguous rule execution

risk_controls_implemented.description: String template validation at rule load time
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleOutcome.validateOutcome() recursively validates all string templates in outcome values using StringTemplates.validate(), checks attribute existence

risk_controls_implemented.description: COPY operator null handling clears destination field
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: COPY operator checks if source attribute is null and removes destination field (context.remove(field)) rather than failing, documented behavior

risk_controls_implemented.description: Type conversion with fault context propagation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: AttributeRegistryImpl.convert() wraps AttributeType.toValue() in try-catch, appends attribute ID to fault subject for traceable error reporting

risk_controls_implemented.description: Default value initialization for missing optional attributes
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleEngine.assertInput() populates default values from AttributeRegistry for missing non-mandatory attributes before validation, ensures complete input context
RuleOutcome.validateCopy() verifies source and destination attribute types match before allowing COPY, prevents silent type mismatches

risk_controls_implemented.description: Metrics and monitoring for rule execution failures
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RemotePartnerRuleRepository reports load failures via Micrometer metrics (rules.load.failure counter per package/partner), enables operational monitoring

risk_controls_implemented.description: Fault aggregation provides comprehensive error reporting
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: RuleEngineFaults supports multiple faults with subject context (fault.type, fault.subject, fault.message), enables tracing errors to specific rule/field/package

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Clinical validation of rule packages before deployment
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Rule engine validates syntax and types but cannot validate clinical correctness (e.g., 'if fever > 38C recommend call112' vs. 'if fever > 39C recommend call112'). Medical review required.

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Operational monitoring and alerting for rule execution failures
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Rule engine provides metrics but cannot respond to failures. System-level monitoring must detect rule load failures, execution errors, and trigger fallback procedures.

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Rule package versioning and rollback capability
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Rule engine loads rules from object storage but does not manage versions or rollback. System must track rule versions and support rollback if new rules cause issues.

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Practitioner training on rule engine limitations and fallback procedures
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Rule engine failures may require manual triage. Practitioners must be trained to recognize when automated triage fails and how to perform manual assessment.

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Integration testing of rule packages in clinical context
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Unit tests validate individual packages but cannot verify correct integration with other triage system components (patient-ui, actions-module). End-to-end testing required at module level.

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Graceful degradation when rule execution fails
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Rule engine throws RuleEngineFaults but cannot implement fallback logic. Calling services must handle failures and provide safe defaults or escalation paths.

failure_modes.name: Expression evaluation exception during rule condition check
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Rule evaluation aborted, RuleEngineFaults thrown with rule ID and error message
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage process interrupted, calling service must handle exception or patient receives no recommendation
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception caught in RuleEngine.when(), logged with rule ID context, metrics incremented
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Calling service must catch RuleEngineFaults and implement fallback (e.g., escalate to practitioner, use conservative default)

failure_modes.name: Mandatory attribute validation failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: RuleEngineFaults thrown before any rule execution, no output produced
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage cannot proceed, patient stuck in interview flow or redirected to manual triage
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: RuleEngine.assertInput() validates all mandatory attributes, collects missing attributes as faults
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Calling service must ensure all mandatory attributes populated before calling rule engine, or implement fallback triage path

failure_modes.name: Immutable field modification attempted
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: RuleEngineFaults thrown immediately when immutable field modification detected, rule execution stops
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Partial triage results, patient may receive incomplete recommendations or require manual intervention
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: AttributeRegistry.isImmutable() checked before field modification, exception thrown with field name
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Rule package must be corrected and redeployed, incident logged for clinical review

failure_modes.name: Attribute constraint violation
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: RuleEngineFaults thrown with constraint violation details (min/max/allowedValues), execution stopped
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage interrupted, potentially mid-execution with partial results
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Validation after each rule execution, checks all constraints on modified attributes
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Depends on when violation occurs: input validation (reject input), output validation (rollback to previous state, log incident)

failure_modes.name: Object storage unavailable during rule loading
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: ObjectStorageException thrown, rule repository initialization fails, service cannot start
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage service unavailable, all patients must use fallback triage path or service is down
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: ObjectStorageClient.getObject() throws exception, logged as error, metrics incremented
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Service restart with retry logic, automatic retry by ObjectStorageRetryingRestClient, manual intervention if persistent

failure_modes.name: Rule package YAML parsing failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: IOException or RuleEngineFaults thrown, specific rule package not loaded
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage functionality depending on that package unavailable, may fall back to system rules or fail
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception logged during loadRules(), metrics counter incremented for package
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Package load failure logged but other packages continue loading, affected partners/functions unavailable until fix deployed

failure_modes.name: Rule verification detects invalid rule syntax
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: RuleEngineFaults thrown with all validation failures (duplicate IDs, invalid operators, type mismatches)
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Rule package rejected, not loaded into repository, clinical functionality unavailable
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: RuleVerifier accumulates all faults, provides detailed error report
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Rule package must be corrected and redeployed, clinical team notified of missing functionality

failure_modes.name: Type conversion failure for attribute value
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: RuleEngineFaults thrown with attribute ID and conversion error details
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage process fails, patient data not processed correctly
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: AttributeType.toValue() catches conversion errors, RuleEngineFaults appends subject context
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Input data correction required or rule package fix needed, manual triage as fallback

failure_modes.name: String template resolution failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Template uses default value or produces malformed text, logged but execution continues
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient/practitioner receives incomplete or generic text instead of personalized message (minor clinical impact)
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Template validation at rule load catches most issues, runtime fallback to default values
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Default values used, incident logged for review, user sees generic text

failure_modes.name: Rule update event processing failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: New rules not loaded, service continues with old rules, error logged
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Triage uses outdated clinical rules until manual intervention or service restart
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception caught in update handler, logged as error, metrics incremented
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Service continues with existing rules, operations team alerted via metrics, manual reload or restart required

failure_modes.name: Multiple rule packages execute in conflicting order
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: All packages execute successfully but later packages override critical clinical decisions from earlier packages
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient receives incorrect recommendation due to rule interaction, not detectable by engine
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: No automatic detection - requires clinical review of rule interactions and package ordering
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Package execution order must be carefully designed, documented, and tested in integration tests

failure_modes.name: COPY operator source attribute is null or missing
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Destination field cleared (removed from context) instead of being populated
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Clinical attribute unexpectedly missing from output (e.g., priority cleared instead of copied from urgency)
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Documented behavior - COPY with null source clears destination, validated in output
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Rule logic must ensure source exists before COPY, or handle missing destination in downstream processing

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: If object storage unavailable or returns corrupted data, rule engine cannot load clinical rules, causing triage service unavailability or execution with outdated/invalid rules
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: ObjectStorageRetryingRestClient provides automatic retry, ObjectStorageException thrown on persistent failure, service initialization fails, metrics incremented
interfaces_with_safety_impact.timeout: Configured in ObjectStorageRetryingRestClient (not visible in this module)

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: If update events fail to deliver or process, service continues using outdated clinical rules after rule updates are deployed, potentially providing obsolete medical guidance
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Exception in event handler logged but does not crash service, old rules remain active, metrics incremented for load failure
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No automatic retry - relies on JMS redelivery or manual intervention

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: If expression parser has bugs or crashes, rule conditions may evaluate incorrectly or throw unexpected exceptions, causing triage to fail or produce wrong recommendations
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Expression.evaluate() exceptions caught in RuleEngine.when(), converted to RuleEngineFaults with rule context, execution stopped
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No retry - failures are fatal for that execution

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: If metrics collection fails, operational visibility lost but triage functionality unaffected. Failure to detect rule load errors or execution problems may delay response to issues.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Metrics calls are optional (meterRegistry null-checked), failures do not interrupt rule processing

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: If attribute registry has incorrect type definitions or constraint configurations, validation may incorrectly accept invalid data or reject valid data, leading to wrong triage outcomes or service failures
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: AttributeRegistry loaded from YAML at startup, validation errors cause service initialization failure, no runtime recovery
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No retry - configuration errors require deployment fix

verification_gaps.description: Rule package ordering and interaction testing incomplete - no tests verify clinical correctness when multiple packages modify same attributes sequentially
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration tests that verify rule package interaction scenarios (e.g., system rules + partner rules, multiple packages setting urgency) with clinical review of expected outcomes

verification_gaps.description: Object storage failure and recovery scenarios not fully tested - tests use local file system, not actual remote storage with failure injection
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add fault injection tests for ObjectStorageClient failures (timeouts, corrupted data, partial reads) and verify metric reporting and retry behavior

verification_gaps.description: Concurrent rule update scenarios not tested - no verification of behavior when rules updated during active execution
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add concurrency tests for rule updates during execution, verify thread-safety of rule repository and absence of race conditions

verification_gaps.description: Metrics reporting accuracy not verified - tests exist but don't verify correct metric values and labels for all failure scenarios
verification_gaps.recommendation: Enhance metric tests to verify exact counter increments, gauge values, and tag correctness for all load failure and execution time scenarios

verification_gaps.description: Expression parser edge cases and error modes not comprehensively tested - reliance on external library without verification of all error paths
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add tests for expression parser edge cases (division by zero, null dereference, type errors) and verify all error modes are handled correctly

verification_gaps.description: Partial rule execution failure scenarios not tested - what happens when rule 5 of 10 fails mid-execution
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add tests for mid-execution failures (immutable violation, constraint violation) and verify output state, error reporting, and no partial state corruption

verification_gaps.description: String template default value handling not fully tested - edge cases with nested templates, missing attributes in complex expressions
verification_gaps.recommendation: Expand string template tests to cover nested templates, chained attribute references, and default value propagation in complex scenarios

cross_item_interfaces: RuleRepository.executePackage() - called by actions-module to execute triage rules
AttributeMap input/output - shared data structure with patient-ui and interviewer services
RuleEngineFaults - exception propagated to calling services for error handling
ObjectStorageClient - shared dependency with other modules for configuration loading
shared_hazards: Object storage unavailable - affects all modules loading configuration from object-storage
Attribute type definition mismatches - if patient-ui and rule-engine have different type schemas
Mandatory attribute missing - if upstream services don't populate required fields
Expression evaluation errors - if expression syntax allowed in multiple modules not consistent
system_level_controls_needed: End-to-end clinical validation of rule packages before production deployment
System-level monitoring dashboard aggregating metrics from rule-engine, actions-module, patient-ui
Graceful degradation strategy when rule engine unavailable (manual triage fallback)
Rule package version control and rollback procedures
Clinical incident review process for rule execution errors affecting patient care
